Name: Nation-State Validator Coercion
Description: Government compels validators in its jurisdiction to censor transactions or implement surveillance
Attack Vector: Legal pressure on incorporated validators; sanctions enforcement; infrastructure seizure
Historical Precedent: OFAC sanctions led 45% of Ethereum blocks to be OFAC-compliant; Tornado Cash addresses censored
Degradation Mode: Transactions from sanctioned addresses delayed or never included; two-tier transaction system emerges
Defense Effectiveness Under Attack: Moderate - geographic distribution helps but major jurisdictions (US/EU) control significant stake

Name: Coordinated Exchange Attack
Description: Major exchanges collude to manipulate oracle prices or halt withdrawals during crisis
Attack Vector: Price manipulation via wash trading; coordinated withdrawal freezes; selective liquidation hunting
Historical Precedent: FTX halted withdrawals while insiders extracted funds; exchange-driven liquidation cascades
Degradation Mode: DeFi protocols using exchange-sourced oracles become manipulation vectors; mass liquidations
Defense Effectiveness Under Attack: Low - multiple oracle sources help but exchanges remain price discovery source

Name: Regulatory Capture of Bridges
Description: Regulators require bridge operators to implement KYC/AML or face shutdown
Attack Vector: Travel rule enforcement on bridge transactions; validator licensing requirements; asset freezing mandates
Historical Precedent: No full precedent but Wormhole and others have identifiable entities; FATF guidance moving this direction
Degradation Mode: Permissionless bridging ends; fragmented liquidity across compliant/non-compliant chains
Defense Effectiveness Under Attack: Very Low - bridges are inherently identifiable chokepoints

Name: Sophisticated Phishing at Scale
Description: AI-generated phishing targeting wallet signers with personalized social engineering
Attack Vector: Deepfake video calls; compromised communication channels; fake governance proposals
Historical Precedent: $600M Ronin hack via compromised validator keys; numerous DAO treasury drains
Degradation Mode: Individual key compromises; slow multisig attacks over time; governance manipulation
Defense Effectiveness Under Attack: Moderate - hardware wallets and social recovery help but human remains weak link

Name: Zero-Day in Cryptographic Primitive
Description: Discovery of fundamental flaw in widely-used cryptographic assumption
Attack Vector: Mathematical breakthrough; quantum computing; implementation bug in core library
Target Primitives: ZK Proofs, FHE, All cryptographic protocols
Historical Precedent: Dual EC DRBG NSA backdoor; various elliptic curve weaknesses discovered over time
Degradation Mode: All dependent systems simultaneously vulnerable; no immediate mitigation possible
Defense Effectiveness Under Attack: Near Zero - cryptographic diversity is limited; most systems share assumptions

Name: Supply Chain Compromise
Description: Malicious code inserted into widely-used dependencies or hardware
Attack Vector: Compromised npm packages; backdoored hardware wallets; infected compiler toolchains
Target Primitives: All smart contract protocols, Key Management, Security Primitives
Historical Precedent: SolarWinds attack; event-stream npm compromise; Ledger data breach
Degradation Mode: Undetected extraction of keys or funds; long-term surveillance; delayed activation
Defense Effectiveness Under Attack: Low - code audits catch some issues but dependencies often unreviewed

Name: Economic Attack on Consensus
Description: Well-funded actor accumulates enough stake/hashpower to manipulate consensus
Attack Vector: 51% attacks; validator bribery; short-selling plus attack combination
Target Primitives: Consensus Mechanisms, Settlement Layers, Data Availability
Historical Precedent: Multiple 51% attacks on smaller PoW chains; theoretical PoS attacks modeled
Degradation Mode: Double-spends; chain reorganizations; censorship; confidence collapse
Defense Effectiveness Under Attack: Moderate for large networks - economic cost is defense but not absolute

Name: Governance Capture via Token Accumulation
Description: Entity quietly accumulates governance tokens to control protocol direction
Attack Vector: OTC accumulation; acquisition of distressed positions; voting bloc coordination
Target Primitives: Governance Primitives, DAO Frameworks, Treasury Management
Historical Precedent: a]16z accumulated significant Uniswap governance power; Curve wars for veCRV control
Degradation Mode: Protocol direction shifts away from decentralization; rent extraction; competitor sabotage
Defense Effectiveness Under Attack: Low - once captured recovery is difficult; token voting inherently plutocratic

Name: Targeted Developer Persecution
Description: Governments arrest or coerce core developers to compromise protocols
Attack Vector: Arrest of key maintainers; travel restrictions; pressure on employers
Historical Precedent: Tornado Cash developer arrested; Roman Sterlingov (Bitcoin Fog) case; Virgil Griffith prosecution
Degradation Mode: Development halts; forced backdoor insertion; protocol abandonment
Defense Effectiveness Under Attack: Moderate - decentralized development helps but key person risk remains

Name: Coordinated Deplatforming
Description: Multiple infrastructure providers simultaneously remove access
Attack Vector: Cloud providers + domain registrars + app stores + payment processors acting together
Target Primitives: All user-facing protocols, Stablecoins (redemption), Fiat Ramps
Historical Precedent: Parler deplatforming; Canadian trucker convoy account freezes; Tornado Cash frontend takedown
Degradation Mode: Protocol still functions but users cannot access; practical death for non-technical users
Defense Effectiveness Under Attack: Low - this is precisely what d/acc infrastructure should defend against but current state is weak

Name: Sybil Attack on Identity Systems
Description: Mass creation of fake identities to subvert proof-of-personhood or quadratic funding
Attack Vector: AI-generated biometrics; purchased identity documents; coordinated false attestations
Target Primitives: Reputation/Sybil Resistance, Quadratic Funding, Governance (if identity-gated)
Historical Precedent: Gitcoin grants farming; World ID biometric spoofing concerns; government ID fraud at scale
Degradation Mode: Democratic mechanisms captured by organized attackers; funding misdirected; governance illegitimate
Defense Effectiveness Under Attack: Moderate - multiple signal aggregation helps but determined attacker can forge most signals

Name: Quantum Computing Breakthrough
Description: Practical quantum computers capable of breaking elliptic curve cryptography
Attack Vector: Shor's algorithm against ECDSA; harvest-now-decrypt-later attacks on recorded transactions
Target Primitives: All cryptographic protocols, Key Management, ZK Proofs (most variants)
Historical Precedent: No precedent but NSA reportedly stockpiling encrypted data; active quantum computing research
Degradation Mode: All current key pairs compromised; historical transactions deanonymizable; need full migration
Defense Effectiveness Under Attack: Moderate for prepared protocols - quantum-resistant alternatives exist but migration is complex

Name: Insider Attack on Protocol Team
Description: Core team member deliberately introduces vulnerability or extracts value
Attack Vector: Backdoor in upgrade; private key theft; social engineering of other team members
Target Primitives: All protocols with privileged access, Centralized sequencers, Upgradeable contracts
Historical Precedent: Multiple rug pulls; insider-assisted hacks; disgruntled employee scenarios
Degradation Mode: Funds drained; trust destroyed; difficult to distinguish from external hack
Defense Effectiveness Under Attack: Low - this is why decentralization matters but many protocols aren't there yet

Name: Smart Contract Bug Exploitation
Description: Discovery and exploitation of logic error in deployed contracts
Attack Vector: Reentrancy; oracle manipulation; flash loan attacks; logic errors
Historical Precedent: The DAO hack; Wormhole; Euler Finance ($197M); Curve pool exploit
Degradation Mode: Protocol funds drained; cascading effects through composability; user fund loss
Defense Effectiveness Under Attack: Low - audits and formal verification help but novel attack vectors emerge constantly
